content.full_text:
July 21, 1976


Honorable Morris J. Stephan (Ret.)
Santa Barbara County
Department No. Sm-2
P.O. Box 1299
Santa Maria, California

Dear Judge Stephan:

     Thank you for your recent inquiry concerning your filing
obligations under the Political Reform Act as a result of recent
legislation which requires that judges of courts of record dis-
close certain financial interests.

     Only persons who are currently serving or have been appointed
or resigned as judges of courts of record since May, 1976 and pos-
sibly retired judges who accept assignments, will be required to
file financial disclosure reports.

     As a judge who retired prior to the effective date of the
lerislation and who does not accept court assignments you will
have no obligation to file a Statement of Economic Interest.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.

[Document text is heavily faded and difficult to read due to the pink/magenta coloring. Most text appears reversed or mirrored, suggesting this may be a carbon copy or reverse image. The legible portions include:]

POU-SP-4

[Several paragraphs of text are present but largely illegible due to the image quality and fading]

[At bottom of page, partially legible text appears to include:]

[illegible]-confidential
[illegible] Classified Information
[illegible]

[Date notation at bottom right, partially visible]

[Note: The majority of this document's content is too faded and unclear to transcribe with confidence. The pink background and reversed/faded text make accurate transcription of the full document content impossible without significant guesswork.]

CHAMBERS OF THE
SUPERIOR COURT
MORRIS J. STEPHAN
JUDGE

Retired

COURT HOUSE

TELEPHONE
WALNUT 2-7709

MAIL ADDRESS
P.O. BOX 1068
91454

P.O. Box 1299

SANTA BARBARA COUNTY
DEPARTMENT NO. SM-2
VETERANS' MEMORIAL BUILDING
RM. 5 TUNNEL
SANTA MARIA, CALIFORNIA

July 13, 1976

The Technical Assistance/Administration
Section of the Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

I do not have available the new legislation concerning
judges filing their financial and property assets. I do not
know whether it applies to retired judges who are receiving
only their retired pay. Since I am drawing no judicial income
other than retired pay, will you kindly advise whether I will
have to comply with the legislation. If so, send me the
necessary forms. Thanking you.

Respectfully yours,

Morris J. Stephan
Morris J. Stephan
Judge of Superior Court
Retired

sjb

[The image appears to be a very faint or low-contrast document. Most of the text is illegible or too faint to read with confidence. There are some extremely faint marks and possibly text in the upper portion and middle sections of the page, but they cannot be reliably transcribed.]

Box 1299 Santa Maria, California Dear Judge Stephan: Thank you for your recent inquiry concerning your filing obligations under the Political Reform Act as a result of recent legislation which requires that judges of courts of record disclose certain financial interests. Only persons who are currently serving or have been appointed or resigned as judges of courts of record since May, 1976 and possibly retired judges who accept assignments, will be required to file financial disclosure reports. As a judge who retired prior to the effective date of the legislation and who does not accept court assignments you will have no obligation to file a Statement of Economic Interest. Very truly yours, Regina Siciliano-Kutchins Technical Assistance Program Coordinator RSK:bw bcc: Jeanne P. The image is a pink-colored sheet of paper with handwritten and typed text. Due to the quality and angle, some parts of the text are partially visible or illegible. Here's what can be reliably extracted: --- Dear Santa Anna, Have no application to file a statement of Common Interest. Legislation and who does not accept court settlements, you will be a judge who retired prior to the effective date of the Federal Recognition Act. We frequently receive inquiries from people who seek services, will be referred to or referred as Judge of courts of record since May 1976 and because our business was currently serving or have need shopping close calls financial interests. I apologize under the Federal Reform Act as a result of recent legislation under the Federal Reform Act. Thank you for your recent inquiry concerning mail filing. Best Wishes Sue M. California P.O. Box 1300 Department No. Gm-5 Santa Barbara County Hospital Road 7 Stebenn (ret) --- This letter appears to be a response to an inquiry about filing a Common Interest statement, explaining that there is no such application due to the person's retirement and involvement in court settlements. The writer expresses appreciation for the inquiry and signs off with best wishes. **CHAMBERS OF THE SUPERIOR COURT** **MORRIS J.
